Title: OsCOMT, encoding a caffeic acid O-methyltransferase in melatonin biosynthesis, increases rice grain yield through dual regulation of leaf senescence and vascular development

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Delays leaf senescence, improves photosynthesis efficiency, promotes vascular development, and increases grain yield in rice
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: final O-methylation step of melatonin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: caffeic acid O-methyltransferase with acetylserotonin O-methyltransferase activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-methylation of N-acetylserotonin to form melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf, sheath and panicle; expression increases in flag leaves from 70 to 110 days post-sowing; high expression in vascular tissues of stems and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf, sheath and panicle; expression increases in flag leaves from 70 to 110 days post-sowing; high expression in vascular tissues of stems and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native OsCOMT promoter for GUS reporter; CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants during grain filling and maturity stages; exogenous melatonin treatment (100 μM to leaves in field; 20 μM to seedlings) used to interpret melatonin-dependent effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression in Nipponbare and Suken118; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli; ELISA for ASMT activity; NAS substrate assay with HPLC melatonin detection; endogenous melatonin quantification; exogenous melatonin rescue; qRT-PCR; GUS reporter; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin accumulation, delays leaf senescence, represses chloroplast and chlorophyll degradation, enhances photosynthesis and vascular bundle development, and increases grain yield per plant and per plot
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCOMT encodes a cytoplasmic caffeic acid O-methyltransferase that exhibits ASMT activity and converts N-acetylserotonin into melatonin in vitro. 2. Overexpression increases endogenous melatonin in flag leaves, whereas CRISPR knockout decreases melatonin, demonstrating an in vivo role in melatonin biosynthesis. 3. OsCOMT delays flag leaf senescence, lowers ROS and cell death, preserves chloroplast structure and chlorophyll, and improves photosynthetic parameters during grain filling. 4. OsCOMT promotes vascular bundle number and size in leaves and culms and is associated with increased trans-zeatin content and altered expression of cytokinin metabolic genes. 5. Overexpression increases grain size, 1000-grain weight, grain number traits and overall grain yield in Nipponbare and also improves yield in the high-yield cultivar Suken118.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for rice yield improvement by overexpression or allele deployment to elevate melatonin, delay senescence, improve source-flow-sink coordination, and enhance yield even in a high-yield variety background
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In Suken118 overexpression lines, seed setting rate was significantly decreased despite net yield increase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although annotated as caffeic acid O-methyltransferase, the paper experimentally demonstrates acetylserotonin O-methyltransferase activity in melatonin biosynthesis. Discussion suggests possible senescence-dependent feedback regulation of OsCOMT expression, but mechanism is unknown.
